evidence: Frames 001.7s–003.5s show the gecko in a distinctly arched/elevated-body posture with lowered hindquarters — the characteristic defecation stance for leopard geckos
Despite a high peak motion ratio (3.784), the gecko stays in the same corner for the entire 8.65s clip — argues against `moving`
Posture visibly relaxes to a flat/lower stance by frame 005.2s, consistent with post-act transition
No food dish, prey, water bowl, or shed skin visible in any frame

`needs_human_review=true` because the top-down infrared angle partially obscures the hindquarters during the arched frames, so the act can't be directly confirmed — within-spot repositioning can't be fully ruled out.
